page: Amazon Web Services m7g.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the ARM64-based AWS Graviton3 processor family, specifically utilizing the Neoverse-V1 core architecture. It features 48 dedicated physical cores running at 2.6 GHz with a single thread per core, backed by 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. It does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory performance. The instance achieves a top-tier rating in PassMark memory testing, supported by uncached read bandwidth of 252.61 GB/sec and write bandwidth of 118.4 GB/sec. Multi-core processing is highly efficient, as demonstrated by strong results in stress-ng multi-core tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and software compilation tasks. Database operations and key-value store performance are also notable, with Redis SET operations reaching 79.78 million operations per second. However, single-core performance and certain single-threaded compression tasks fall within the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and strong multi-core throughput per unit of power and resource allocation compared to traditional x86 alternatives. The lack of bundled local storage means storage-heavy workloads must rely on network-attached options, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. This instance is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation pipelines, static web serving, redis caching layers, and medium-scale database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services m7g.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the ARM64 AWS Graviton3 processor, featuring 48 dedicated physical cores at 2.6 GHz and 192.0 GB of system memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network speed of 22 Gbps without local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core execution, particularly in software compilation, database operations, and Redis caching, where it handles 79.78 million SET operations per second. While single-core performance remains average, the dedicated core allocation and high memory bandwidth provide excellent resource density. This instance is highly suited for multi-threaded workloads, web serving, and containerized microservices.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.12xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 instance featuring 48 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.12xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 instance with 48 dedicated Graviton3 cores and 192 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated ARM64 general-purpose instance delivering high memory bandwidth and strong multi-core performance for scale-out workloads and database applications.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory with top-tier bandwidth performance
22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core execution for software compilation and database operations
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
